what_it_does: Exports a small set of reusable React UI primitives (SectionHead, LaneHead, Badge, Surface, EmptyState) plus a Tone union type, intended to give every product region a shared visual language via token-only kit-* CSS classes.
observations: All styling is delegated to kit-* utility classes; no literal CSS values appear in the file.
LaneHead dynamically renders as a <button> when onToggle is provided, otherwise as a <div>.
Surface forwards a data-ui-ref attribute directly onto its root element for UI-target resolution.
Tone is a closed union of five semantic lifecycle/status strings: sig, await, fail, wire, dim.
SectionHead always uses an <h3> and supports an uppercase kicker plus a right-aligned aside slot.
EmptyState is a single-purpose wrapper with no additional props beyond children.
standards: The file is entirely presentational primitives with no business logic or side effects.
Comments frame the components as a 'one language for the whole product' design-system vocabulary.
conventions: Props are destructured inline with explicit types rather than separate interfaces.
Class names are concatenated with string arithmetic, guarded by conditional flags.
Semantic tone is encoded by a kit-tone-<tone> modifier class shared across multiple components.
ReactNode is imported as a type-only import.
concerns: Surface renders an onClick handler on a <div> without adding role='button' or keyboard handling, which can hurt accessibility unless callers supply it.
LaneHead as a button does not expose aria-expanded, so the open/expand state is only communicated visually by the caret.
The caret symbols are swapped relative to common convention (open shows ⌄, closed shows ⌃); this may be intentional but is worth verifying.
SectionHead hardcodes <h3>, which may conflict with the surrounding document heading hierarchy.
notes: A token-driven React component kit that translates design-system CSS classes into a small set of region-building primitives.
questions: Where are the kit-* and kit-tone-* CSS rules defined, and are they linted against off-token values?
Is data-ui-ref consumed by a runtime resolver elsewhere, and does it expect a specific format?
Should LaneHead expose aria-expanded and aria-controls when used as a collapsible band?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide reusable, recognition-by-sight building blocks for product regions; the file does exactly that, but leaves accessibility and styling enforcement to callers and external CSS.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a single, consistent component dialect across the product so that every region feels like the same interface rather than a collection of local styles.
novelty: The components are deliberately minimal and named around operational concepts (LaneHead, Surface) rather than generic UI names, suggesting a domain-shaped design system.
contribution: A compact, reusable set of presentational primitives and a tone taxonomy that downstream regions can compose without inventing their own markup or class names.
summary_for_embedding: A React shared-kit module exporting five presentational primitives and a Tone type, styled through token-only kit-* CSS classes for consistent product-region vocabulary.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a unified, sight-recognizable set of building blocks that regions compose into a coherent interface language.

symbols.does: Declares a closed union of five semantic tone tokens used to drive colour and status across kit components.
symbols.description: Tone is defined as 'sig' | 'await' | 'fail' | 'wire' | 'dim'. It acts as the shared status/lifecycle vocabulary that components map to kit-tone-* CSS modifiers, enabling consistent visual signalling without literal colour values in code.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/components/kit.tsx::SectionHead | component | export function SectionHead({ children, tag, aside }: { children: ReactNode; tag?: string; aside?: ReactNode }) {
symbols.does: Renders a region or panel heading with an optional uppercase kicker and a right-aligned aside slot.
symbols.description: SectionHead is the display-voice title primitive. It wraps a mandatory <h3> inside kit-sechead, optionally prefixed by a kit-kicker tag and followed by a kit-sechead-aside node, giving every panel a single recognizable heading shape.

symbols.code_id:
code://company/canvas/app/src/components/kit.tsx::LaneHead | component | export function LaneHead({ children, count, tone = 'dim', onToggle, open }:
  { children: ReactNode; count?: number; tone?: Tone; onToggle?: () => void; open?: boolean }) {
symbols.does: Renders a grouped-lane header with a tone tint, name, optional count, and optional collapse affordance.
symbols.description: LaneHead renders as a button when onToggle is provided, otherwise as a div. It applies a kit-tone-* class to communicate the lane's meaning visually, shows a caret that flips with open, and displays a count badge, serving as the header for triage-style lanes.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/components/kit.tsx::Badge | component | export function Badge({ children, tone = 'dim' }: { children: ReactNode; tone?: Tone }) {
symbols.does: Renders a small status pill whose colour and shape communicate state.
symbols.description: Badge is a minimal span applying kit-badge and a kit-tone-* modifier. It is designed for recognition-by-sight: the tone class encodes lifecycle state such as awaiting, done, or held without relying on text alone.

symbols.code_id:
code://company/canvas/app/src/components/kit.tsx::Surface | component | export function Surface({ children, tone, onClick, className = '', title, interactive, dataUiRef }:
  { children: ReactNode; tone?: Tone; onClick?: () => void; className?: string; title?: string; interactive?: boolean; dataUiRef?: string }) {
symbols.does: Renders an elevated card-like row with optional accent spine, tone tint, click handling, and a UI-reference attribute.
symbols.description: Surface is the card primitive for list-as-navigable-form patterns. It composes class names to add tone spine colouring and an actionable hover look when onClick or interactive is supplied, and it forwards data-ui-ref unchanged so external UI-target resolvers can address the element.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/components/kit.tsx::EmptyState | component | export function EmptyState({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
symbols.does: Renders a plain honest 'nothing here' message.
symbols.description: EmptyState is a single-purpose wrapper using the kit-empty class. It exists to prevent blank gaps by always stating the rest-state, aligning with a fail-loud-friendly design stance.
